title: Caching strategy and reruns
text: nlmixr2targets builds targets, caches every target keyed hash command, dependencies, inputs. call tar_make(), targets whose hash changed (directly transitively) rebuilt. single tar_nlmixr(name = <name>, ...) call, four targets generated : single important property layout <name>_fit_simple depends structural content model. nlmixr_object_simplify() strips parameter labels model’s metadata environment caching, cosmetic edits either invalidate expensive estimation step.

title: Caching strategy and reruns
code: # Adding a comment, changing a label, or editing meta: #   only re-runs the cheap `_object_simple` and the final `<name>` target #   (the relabel/meta-restore step). The expensive `_fit_simple` is reused.  # Changing initial values (`ini({...})`) or the model structure # (`model({...})`): #   re-runs everything from `_object_simple` onward.  # Adding/removing a covariate column from the dataset: #   re-runs `_data_simple` and `_fit_simple`. `_object_simple` is reused #   if the model didn't change.  # Changing `est` or `control`: #   re-runs `_fit_simple` and the final target. `_object_simple` and #   `_data_simple` are reused.

what: Inspecting and pruning the indirect cache
title: Caching strategy and reruns
text: simplified-model md5 cache lives <tar_config_get("store")>/user/nlmixr2/. removed targets::tar_destroy(), can accumulate orphan files time (e.g. model iterations). nlmixr2targets_cache_prune() keep argument reads targets::tar_meta() current store, finds every built target whose name ends _object_simple, treats hash values reachable. Anything else cache directory reported (, dry_run = FALSE, removed). Pass keep = character() wipe cache unconditionally.
code: # Quick inventory: hashes, sizes, last-modified nlmixr2targets_cache_status() #>      hash size_bytes               mtime #> 1 aaaaaa..       1234 2026-05-21 12:00:00 #> 2 bbbbbb..       1567 2026-05-21 13:30:00  # Find orphans (dry run by default) nlmixr2targets_cache_prune()  # Delete them nlmixr2targets_cache_prune(dry_run = FALSE)

Using nlmixr2targets requires use targets package. learn targets package, see targets website.

what: Running one model with one dataset (tar_nlmixr())
title: Estimating nlmixr2 models with 'nlmixr2targets'
text: tar_nlmixr() function allows estimate one model one dataset. generate three targets: simplified version model, minimal version dataset, estimation step. simplified version model removes parts less reproducible changes none model intent. (Advanced information: parts removed source references model name. Also, model modified step setting initial values described previous section vignette.)
code: library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(nlmixr2targets)  pheno <- function() {   ini({     lcl <- log(0.008); label("Typical value of clearance")     lvc <- log(0.6); label("Typical value of volume of distribution")     etalcl + etalvc ~ c(1,                         0.01, 1)     cpaddSd <- 0.1; label("residual variability")   })   model({     cl <- exp(lcl + etalcl)     vc <- exp(lvc + etalvc)     kel <- cl / vc     d / dt(central) <- -kel * central     cp <- central / vc     cp ~ add(cpaddSd)   }) }  plan_model <-   tar_plan(     myData = nlmixr2data::pheno_sd,     tar_nlmixr(       model_pheno,       object = pheno,       data = myData,       est = "saem"     )   )  list(   plan_model )

what: Running multiple models with one dataset (tar_nlmixr_multimodel())
title: Estimating nlmixr2 models with 'nlmixr2targets'
text: common use case generate multiple models using single dataset estimation method. tar_nlmixr_multimodel() allows generation named list models allow subsequent analysis models. Internally, tar_nlmixr_multimodel() passes models tar_nlmixr() data set simplification equivalent steps run per model, model run often required dataset model changes.
code: library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(nlmixr2targets)  pheno <- function() {   ini({     lcl <- log(0.008); label("Typical value of clearance")     lvc <- log(0.6); label("Typical value of volume of distribution")     etalcl + etalvc ~ c(1,                         0.01, 1)     cpaddSd <- 0.1; label("residual variability")   })   model({     cl <- exp(lcl + etalcl)     vc <- exp(lvc + etalvc)     kel <- cl / vc     d / dt(central) <- -kel * central     cp <- central / vc     cp ~ add(cpaddSd)   }) }  pheno2 <- function() {   ini({     lcl <- log(0.008); label("Typical value of clearance")     lvc <- log(0.6); label("Typical value of volume of distribution")     etalcl + etalvc ~ c(2,                         0.01, 2)     cpaddSd <- 3.0; label("residual variability")   })   model({     cl <- exp(lcl + etalcl)     vc <- exp(lvc + etalvc)     kel <- cl / vc     d / dt(central) <- -kel * central     cp <- central / vc     cp ~ add(cpaddSd)   }) }  plan_model <-   tar_nlmixr_multimodel(     all_models,     data = nlmixr2data::pheno_sd,     est = "saem",     "Base model; additive residual error = 1" = pheno,     "Base model; additive residual error = 3" = pheno2   )  plan_report <-   tar_plan(     # Determine the AIC for all tested models     aic_list = sapply(X = all_models, FUN = AIC)   )  list(   plan_model,   plan_report )

code: library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(nlmixr2targets) library(nlmixr2)  pheno <- function() {   ini({     lcl <- log(0.008); label("Typical value of clearance")     lvc <- log(0.6); label("Typical value of volume of distribution")     etalcl + etalvc ~ c(1,                         0.01, 1)     cpaddSd <- 0.1; label("residual variability")   })   model({     cl <- exp(lcl + etalcl)     vc <- exp(lvc + etalvc)     kel <- cl / vc     d / dt(central) <- -kel * central     cp <- central / vc     cp ~ add(cpaddSd)   }) }  plan_model <-   tar_nlmixr_multimodel(     all_models,     data = nlmixr2data::pheno_sd,     est = "saem",     "Base model; additive residual error = 1" = pheno,     "Base model; additive residual error = 3" =     all_models[["Base model; additive residual error = 1"]] |> ini(cpaddSd = 3)   )  list(   plan_model )

what: Continuing the pipeline when a model fails
title: Estimating nlmixr2 models with 'nlmixr2targets'
text: default, model fails estimation error propagates targets::tar_make() stops, just target error . usually want single model, fitting many models (example tar_nlmixr_multimodel()) one failing model otherwise halt whole pipeline prevent seeing models succeed. tar_nlmixr() tar_nlmixr_multimodel() accept error argument control : error = "stop" (default) lets estimation error propagate halt pipeline. error = "continue" catches estimation error stores failure sentinel target instead, rest pipeline still runs. sentinel object class nlmixr2targetsError also inherits "try-error", carries original error message. clearly nlmixr2 fit object, can detect failed model simple inherits() check. tar_make(), target failed estimate can inspected directly:
code: library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(nlmixr2targets)  plan_model <-   tar_nlmixr_multimodel(     all_models,     data = nlmixr2data::pheno_sd,     est = "saem",     error = "continue",     "Base model; additive residual error = 1" = pheno,     "Base model; additive residual error = 3" = pheno2   )  plan_report <-   tar_plan(     # Keep only the models that estimated successfully     successful_models = Filter(       f = function(fit) !inherits(fit, "try-error"),       x = all_models     ),     # Compute AIC for the successful models only     aic_list = sapply(X = successful_models, FUN = AIC)   )  list(   plan_model,   plan_report ) fit <- tar_read(all_models)[["Base model; additive residual error = 3"]] inherits(fit, "try-error")  # TRUE if this model failed print(fit)                  # shows the captured error message

title: Initial conditions cheatsheet
text: native nlmixr2 DSL syntax compartment initial value cmt(0) <- value inside model({...}) block. Inside tar_nlmixr() tar_nlmixr_multimodel() may also write cmt(initial) <- value, nlmixr2targets translates back cmt(0) <- value nlmixr2 ever sees model. cmt(initial) form workaround exists use nlmixr2targets; form nlmixr2 understands, fail call nlmixr2est::nlmixr() directly model still contains cmt(initial). forms produce fit routed tar_nlmixr():
code: # Form 1: native nlmixr2 DSL syntax (works in nlmixr2 directly and # inside nlmixr2targets) model({   d / dt(central) <- -kel * central   central(0) <- 0          # initial value at time 0   cp <- central / vc   cp ~ add(cpaddSd) })  # Form 2: `cmt(initial) <- value` -- a `nlmixr2targets`-only # workaround. Use this only when something prevents the natural # `central(0) <- 0` form from being walked by `targets` (see the # "Known limitation" section below). `nlmixr2targets` rewrites this # back to `central(0) <- 0` before passing the model to nlmixr2. model({   d / dt(central) <- -kel * central   # nlmixr2targets-only; not understood by bare nlmixr2   central(initial) <- 0   cp <- central / vc   cp ~ add(cpaddSd) })

title: Multimodel comparison workflows
text: tar_nlmixr_multimodel() lets declare several candidate models dataset one call. model gets simplify/fit/relabel chain, data-simplification step shared possible, within-list piping (models[[""]] |> ini(...) referenced another entry) resolved dependency prior fit data re-prepared. output list targets can drop _targets.R plan.

title: Multimodel comparison workflows
text: tar_make(), aic_table target gives compact summary candidates. Add dAIC weight columns taste.
code: library(targets) library(tarchetypes) library(nlmixr2targets)  pheno_base <- function() {   ini({     lcl <- log(0.008); label("Typical clearance")     lvc <- log(0.6); label("Typical volume of distribution")     etalcl + etalvc ~ c(1, 0.01, 1)     cpaddSd <- 0.1; label("Additive residual SD")   })   model({     cl <- exp(lcl + etalcl)     vc <- exp(lvc + etalvc)     kel <- cl / vc     d / dt(central) <- -kel * central     cp <- central / vc     cp ~ add(cpaddSd)   }) }  list(   tar_nlmixr_multimodel(     name = candidate_fits,     data = nlmixr2data::pheno_sd,     est  = "saem",     "Base"                              = pheno_base,     "Base + tighter residual prior"     = pheno_base |> ini(cpaddSd = 0.05),     "Base + alternate residual"         = pheno_base |> model({       cp ~ prop(cpaddSd)     }, append = TRUE)   ),   tar_target(     aic_table,     data.frame(       model = names(candidate_fits),       aic   = vapply(candidate_fits, AIC, numeric(1)),       bic   = vapply(candidate_fits, BIC, numeric(1)),       ofv   = vapply(candidate_fits, function(f) f$objDf$OBJF[1], numeric(1))     )   ) )

title: Targets for nlmixr2 Pipelines
text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: library(targets) targets::tar_script({   library(nlmixr2targets)   pheno <- function() {     ini({       tcl <- log(0.008) # typical value of clearance       tv <-  log(0.6)   # typical value of volume       ## var(eta.cl)       eta.cl + eta.v ~ c(1,                          0.01, 1) ## cov(eta.cl, eta.v), var(eta.v)       # interindividual variability on clearance and volume       add.err <- 0.1    # residual variability     })     model({       cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl) # individual value of clearance       v <- exp(tv + eta.v)    # individual value of volume       ke <- cl / v            # elimination rate constant       d/dt(A1) = - ke * A1    # model differential equation       cp = A1 / v             # concentration in plasma       cp ~ add(add.err)       # define error model     })   }   list(     tar_nlmixr(name=pheno_model, object=pheno, data=nlmixr2data::pheno_sd, est="saem")   ) }) targets::tar_make()

what: Breaking changes from unreleased development version
title: nlmixr2targets 0.1.0
text: tar_nlmixr() now produces intermediate targets named <name>_object_simple, <name>_data_simple, <name>_fit_simple (single-underscore separator). Prior versions used longer <name>_tar_object_simple/_tar_data_simple/_tar_fit_simple pattern. new names align convention already used tar_nlmixr_multimodel(). Users cached pipelines earlier versions see one rebuild (old targets become orphans); targets::tar_destroy() remove desired.
